tasks.title: Infra - Supabase Storage bucket setup and misokinesia stimuli verification
tasks.summary: Provision the public misokinesia storage bucket and verify the seeded stimulus metadata for launch.
tasks.description: Set up the public Supabase Storage bucket `misokinesia-stimuli`, upload placeholder or final mp4 clips, and verify that the existing seed script populates one active test set plus 29 active stimuli rows with the expected storage paths, durations, and sort order. Confirm that the public CDN URLs resolve without authentication using the documented `{SUPABASE_URL}/storage/v1/object/public/misokinesia-stimuli/{storage_path}` pattern.
acceptance_criteria: `misokinesia-stimuli` Supabase Storage bucket exists with public access enabled
At least one placeholder or final mp4 is uploaded and its public URL is directly accessible in a browser with no auth
`backend/admin_cli/seed_misokinesia_stimuli.py` exists and can seed the misokinesia test set plus 29 stimuli rows
Running the seed inserts exactly 1 row in `misokinesia_test_sets` and 29 rows in `misokinesia_stimuli`
All 29 stimuli rows have correct `sort_order`, `storage_path`, and `duration_ms` values
POST `/misokinesia/start` returns manifest URLs that load immediately with no 403 or redirect
notes: The seed script already exists in the repo; the remaining work is external bucket provisioning, asset upload, and verification.

tasks.title: Analytics v2 - Contract scaffolding and version bump
tasks.summary: Define the backend/frontend analytics v2 contract for day-level temperature summaries before modeling or UI changes land.
tasks.description: Update shared analytics constants and typed response models to introduce the `dashboard-analytics-v2` / `weather-mlm-v2` contract plus a scaffolded `temperature_summary` payload. Limit this patch to constants, backend Pydantic schemas, frontend API typings, and schema-level tests. Do not change mixed-model logic, snapshot orchestration, or dashboard rendering in this task.
acceptance_criteria: `backend/app/analytics/constants.py` defines `ANALYTICS_RESPONSE_VERSION=\"dashboard-analytics-v2\"` and `ANALYTICS_MODEL_VERSION=\"weather-mlm-v2\"`
`backend/app/schemas/analytics.py` includes typed models for `temperature_summary`, summary windows, frequency bins, and hot/cold group payloads
`frontend/src/lib/api/index.ts` matches the new backend response shape including `temperature_summary`
`backend/tests/test_analytics_schema.py` is updated to serialize a valid scaffolded v2 payload
notes: Keep this task schema-only so later implementation tasks can build on a stable typed contract.
Do not wire `temperature_summary` into live responses yet.

tasks.title: Analytics v2 - Day-level weather z-score parity
tasks.summary: Refactor mixed-model preparation so day-level weather predictors are standardized by unique study day rather than repeated participant rows.
tasks.description: Change the analytics modeling path so `temperature`, `precipitation`, and `daylight_hours` are z-scored across unique `date_local` values in the complete-case sample for each outcome, then mapped back onto participant rows before fitting. Keep participant-level predictors and outcomes standardized on participant rows, and leave the mixed-model formulas unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: For each outcome, weather predictor z-scores are computed from unique complete-case study days only
`anxiety`, `depression`, `loneliness`, `self_report`, and `digit_span_score` continue to standardize across participant rows in the complete-case sample
Uneven participant counts per day no longer change the weather z-score assigned to a given day
Existing model formulas and random intercept grouping by `date_bin` remain unchanged
Regression tests cover repeated-day weighting and zero-variance warning behavior
notes: Do not add the temperature summary payload in this task.
Avoid changing response schemas or route-handler code here.

tasks.title: Analytics v2 - Temperature summary engine
tasks.summary: Build a standalone backend helper that computes overall and seasonal day-level temperature summaries, histograms, and hot/cold groups.
tasks.description: Add a dedicated analytics helper module for `temperature_summary` that works from the canonical analytics dataset rows without changing the mixed-model path. The helper should generate `overall`, `fall_winter`, and `spring_summer` windows, count temperature frequencies by unique day using 1°C bins, and compute strict hot/cold groups from window-specific day-level temperature z-scores.
acceptance_criteria: A standalone backend helper returns `overall`, `fall_winter`, and `spring_summer` summary windows from canonical analytics dataset rows
Frequency bins count unique days rather than participant rows
`cold_group` uses `temperature_z < -2` and `hot_group` uses `temperature_z > 2` within each window
Empty seasonal windows or empty hot/cold groups serialize as zero-count payloads rather than errors
Regression coverage encodes the current workbook oracle counts and dates documented in `docs/labs/weather-wellness/ANALYTICS.md`
notes: Keep this task focused on the summary engine and its tests only; wiring into snapshot/live responses happens later.
If a new test file is created, keep fixture data minimal and deterministic.

tasks.title: Analytics v2 - Snapshot and route integration
tasks.summary: Wire the v2 temperature summary into backend snapshot/live responses and the same-origin analytics route/cache layer.
tasks.description: Integrate the new summary helper into the analytics service so `temperature_summary` appears in ready/stale/recomputing responses and round-trips through snapshot persistence. Update the same-origin Route Handler and cache key namespace to serve the v2 payload shape without mixing old cached snapshot data into new responses.
acceptance_criteria: `backend/app/services/analytics_service.py` includes `temperature_summary` in the returned analytics payload and persisted snapshot payloads
Snapshot and live responses continue to behave correctly for `ready`, `stale`, `recomputing`, `insufficient_data`, and `failed` states
The same-origin analytics route uses a v2 cache key namespace so old v1 cached payloads are not reused
Backend service/router tests and frontend route-handler tests are updated for the new field and version strings
notes: Do not add new dashboard UI in this task.
Preserve the existing snapshot-miss and background refresh behavior.

tasks.title: Analytics v2 - Minimal dashboard temperature summary UI
tasks.summary: Render a minimal RA-facing temperature summary card in the analytics section using the new v2 payload.
tasks.description: Add a compact dashboard UI for `temperature_summary` beneath the existing analytics cards/effect plot. Keep the initial UI simple: selector tabs for `overall`, `fall_winter`, and `spring_summer`; a small summary strip; a day-level frequency-vs-temperature chart or histogram; and hot/cold group panels listing counts and qualifying dates. Do not redesign the broader dashboard in this task.
acceptance_criteria: `DashboardAnalyticsSection` renders temperature summary controls for `overall`, `fall_winter`, and `spring_summer` when `temperature_summary` is present
The selected window shows day count, participant count, mean temperature, standard deviation, and hot/cold participant counts
The UI includes a simple day-level frequency-vs-temperature visualization based on `frequency_bins`
Hot and cold groups render qualifying dates and participant counts, and show a benign empty state when a group has no days
Existing model cards, status panels, and effect plot behavior remain intact
notes: Keep this as a first-pass UI; visual polish can follow in a later task.
Do not move or merge this summary into the weather time-series chart.
